title: Reasons for rejection during the application process

groups.question_title: Which TWO aspects should people just graduating from secondary schools emphasise?
groups.desc:
Questions 21 and 22
Choose TWO letters, A-E.

list.title: E
list.content: other areas of interests outside work

groups.question_title: Which TWO of the following are mentioned about women who returned to work after raising children?
groups.desc:
Questions 23 and 24
Choose TWO letters, A-E.

list.title: B
list.content: They are more expensive to retrain.

list.title: E
list.content: They have difficulty in re-adjusting to work.

groups.question_title: Which TWO types of people do employers always avoid hiring?
groups.desc:
Questions 25 and 26
Choose TWO letters, A-E.

list.title: A
list.content: people who have worked for a rival company

list.title: B
list.content: people who have worked for their previous company for a long time

list.title: C
list.content: people who were sacked by a previous company

list.title: D
list.content: people who have been unemployed for a long time

list.title: E
list.content: people who quit their job because it was unsuitable

groups.question_title: Which THREE factors are mentioned as reasons why people avoid hiring retrenched workers?

list.title: A
list.content: lack of experience relevant to new industries

list.title: F
list.content: the reputation of the previous company

groups.title: Reasons for rejection during the application process
groups.desc:
Question 30
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for the answer.
groups.collect:
• unsuitable experience and skills
• applicants' inappropriate $30$ and appearance
• poor social skills

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: For today's session we're going to look at the job market and employers' hiring strategies.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: It mainly takes great people to generate and execute efficient strategies and ideas.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: In fact, a company's growth is strongly supported by its human resources.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Therefore, we'll look at the determining factors for recruitment, the traits of a successful applicant and the possible reasons for rejection.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: First, let's take a look at the job market for students who have just graduated from secondary schools.

transcript.speaker: Sansa
transcript.en: So, what should they focus on when applying for a job?

transcript.speaker: Sansa
transcript.en: Is education the most important factor in recruitment?

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Well, actually the degree is not that important.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: It's not the only factor that personnel managers take into consideration.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Some employees with years of work experience still have a competitive edge over fresh graduates with a higher educational degree.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: However, with neither an impressive educational background nor practical experience, secondary school graduates should stress that they have great enthusiasm for work.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Employees who are interested in their job often work hard and are willing to take more responsibilities.

transcript.speaker: Sansa
transcript.en: What else should they highlight during a job interview?

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Well, it is wise for them to demonstrate that they possess valuable characteristics such as curiosity, willingness to learn new stuff and good interpersonal skills.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Companies favour applicants who are easy going and fit in quickly.

transcript.speaker: Sansa
transcript.en: Right.

transcript.speaker: Sansa
transcript.en: But I guess things get harder for women to re-enter the workforce and reconnect after raising children at home.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Absolutely.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: After taking an extended absence from work, women with young children have to strike a good balance between work and family.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Changes have to be made within the family to ensure they have sufficient working hours.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Apart from that, they are working with totally new colleagues in an unfamiliar workplace.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: After even two or three years of absence, they face challenges of rusty or obsolete skills.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Even though they're quite confident in themselves, they often find it hard to adapt to these changes.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: But on the other hand, many employers find these mature women valuable assets in the workplace.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: They are excellent role models for younger employees as being quite motivated and with a strong work ethic.

transcript.speaker: Sansa
transcript.en: Umm … Indeed, employers not only need qualified but also flexible and well-trained employees.

transcript.speaker: Sansa
transcript.en: Given the experience, perspectives and stability, these women are offered unique opportunities in today's workplace.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Yes, exactly.

transcript.speaker: Sansa
transcript.en: So what kind of people do companies generally find unwelcoming?

transcript.speaker: Sansa
transcript.en: Will they consider candidates from a rival company?

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Well, actually, hiring employees from a competing firm is not a taboo.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Highly skilled workers are always welcomed no matter where they're from.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: What concerns employers the most is how they left the previous company that they had worked for years.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: If they have been fired, they must have done something wrong during the previous job.

transcript.speaker: Sansa
transcript.en: What about people who remain unemployed for a considerable period of time?

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Well, this type of employee isn't at the bottom of the employment list.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: The reason why they remain unemployed is the key.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Most companies cast doubts on applicants who left the job simply because they didn't like the working hours or the work assigned.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Have you read any articles about worker retrenchment?

transcript.speaker: Sansa
transcript.en: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Sansa
transcript.en: Worker retrenchment on a massive scale is sometimes introduced as one of the short-term measures to resuscitate the industry through the reduction of overhead costs.

transcript.speaker: Sansa
transcript.en: Workers are also laid-off as companies restructure and reform.

transcript.speaker: Sansa
transcript.en: But what happens to these retrenched workers afterwards?

transcript.speaker: Sansa
transcript.en: Is it easy for them to find another job?

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Well, generally companies are reluctant to hire them.

transcript.speaker: Sansa
transcript.en: Why is that?

transcript.speaker: Sansa
transcript.en: They must have plenty of work experience in the previous industry.

transcript.speaker: Sansa
transcript.en: Isn't that a strength for them in the job market?

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Actually, new industries are eager to hire experienced workers, but retrenched workers know nothing about other industries.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: And it also costs employers more to retrain them.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: They need training courses to acquire relevant skills and qualifications for a new career.

transcript.speaker: Sansa
transcript.en: Our government is providing public training programs to help them identify their current skills and acquire new skills to improve their career options.

transcript.speaker: Sansa
transcript.en: Isn't that right?

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: But retrenched workers are generally too old.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: They are less motivated and energized at work.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Companies thus prefer younger employees to them.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: They are also reluctant to adjust and are unwilling to be retrained.

transcript.speaker: Sansa
transcript.en: Do retrenched workers from companies with big names have an advantage over other job applicants?

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: No.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Employers today value skills more than where they're previously from.

transcript.speaker: Sansa
transcript.en: Right.

transcript.speaker: Sansa
transcript.en: So what are the most common factors of rejection during application?

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Applicants are rejected mainly for three reasons.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: First, they have experience and skills that are irrelevant to the industry.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Even experienced workers may receive rejection letters.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Wearing appropriate clothes and how they look during an interview are key to a successful interview.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: No matter how good their presentation skills are, a shirt with stains or informal clothes like a pair of jeans only means the applicants aren't taking the interview seriously.

transcript.speaker: Sansa
transcript.en: Yes, during an interview, formal dress is key to a good start and will inevitably affect how people evaluate you later.

transcript.speaker: Sansa
transcript.en: What's the third factor?

transcript.speaker: Sansa
transcript.en: Let me guess, is it interpersonal skills?

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Companies are eager to hire people who can work well with others and fit in quickly.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: It's difficult to work in an environment full of conflicts.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Therefore, people who don't get along well with others are often rejected.

transcript.speaker: Sansa
transcript.en: I see …

21.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 secondary schools；提示词 emphasise。
题目解析：定位词 secondary schools 出现后，先后听到提示词 emphasise 的同义替换 focus on 和与选项 D 中 education 相关的信息出现在学生的提问中，但导师以 the degree is not that important 予以否定，the degree 对应选项 D 的 educational qualifications，故排除。接着听到了选项 B practical experience 的同义替换 work experience，但此处提及的工作经验与高中毕业生无关，且随后出现了转折信号词 However，从而可排除选项 B，重点听该之后的内容。之后的内容中夹杂了一个否定结构 neither … nor … 连接的选项 D 和 B，由此确定这两个选项都不是正确答案，真正的答案信息为：“… secondary school graduates should stress that they have great enthusiasm for work. Employees who are interested in their job …”其中 stress 替换了提示词 emphasise，great enthusiasm 和 are interested in 替换了选项 A 的 degree of motivation（积极性），故选项 A 正确。而选项 E 偷换概念，这句话只提到了对工作感兴趣，并未提及其他工作之外的领域，可排除。之后学生的提问“What else should they highlight …”，highlight 替换了提示词 emphasise，暗示另一个答案即将出现，注意听导师的回答：“… it is wise for them to demonstrate that they possess valuable characteristics such as curiosity, willingness to learn new stuff and good interpersonal skills.”其中 willingness to learn new stuff 替换了选项 C 的 eagerness to learn，故选项 C 正确。因此答案为 A & C。

22.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 secondary schools；提示词 emphasise。
题目解析：定位词 secondary schools 出现后，先后听到提示词 emphasise 的同义替换 focus on 和与选项 D 中 education 相关的信息出现在学生的提问中，但导师以 the degree is not that important 予以否定，the degree 对应选项 D 的 educational qualifications，故排除。接着听到了选项 B practical experience 的同义替换 work experience，但此处提及的工作经验与高中毕业生无关，且随后出现了转折信号词 However，从而可排除选项 B，重点听该之后的内容。之后的内容中夹杂了一个否定结构 neither … nor … 连接的选项 D 和 B，由此确定这两个选项都不是正确答案，真正的答案信息为：“… secondary school graduates should stress that they have great enthusiasm for work. Employees who are interested in their job …”其中 stress 替换了提示词 emphasise，great enthusiasm 和 are interested in 替换了选项 A 的 degree of motivation（积极性），故选项 A 正确。而选项 E 偷换概念，这句话只提到了对工作感兴趣，并未提及其他工作之外的领域，可排除。之后学生的提问“What else should they highlight …”，highlight 替换了提示词 emphasise，暗示另一个答案即将出现，注意听导师的回答：“… it is wise for them to demonstrate that they possess valuable characteristics such as curiosity, willingness to learn new stuff and good interpersonal skills.”其中 willingness to learn new stuff 替换了选项 C 的 eagerness to learn，故选项 C 正确。因此答案为 A & C。

23.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 women、after raising children。
题目解析：本题定位词原词重现，定位后首先听到了与选项 A 中 working time 相关的信息：“… Changes have to be made within the family to ensure they have sufficient working hours.”不过这句以及之前那句都是在说需要照顾小孩的女性应当如何调整在家庭和工作上的时间，以取得二者之间的平衡，并没有说她们在工作上时间有限（limited），属于偷换概念，可排除。两三句无效信息后，听到：“Even though they're quite confident in themselves, they often find it hard to adapt to these changes.”其中 quite confident in themselves 与选项 D 中的 lack confidence 矛盾，hard to adapt to 替换了选项 E 中的 difficulty in re-adjusting，故选项 D 错误，选项 E 正确。最后听到了由 But on the other hand 引导出的另一正确答案信息：“… many employers find these mature women valuable assets in the workplace.”其中出现了选项 C 中的 mature，故选项 C 正确。选项 B 在录音中导师并未提及。因此答案为 C & E。

24.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 women、after raising children。
题目解析：本题定位词原词重现，定位后首先听到了与选项 A 中 working time 相关的信息：“… Changes have to be made within the family to ensure they have sufficient working hours.”不过这句以及之前那句都是在说需要照顾小孩的女性应当如何调整在家庭和工作上的时间，以取得二者之间的平衡，并没有说她们在工作上时间有限（limited），属于偷换概念，可排除。两三句无效信息后，听到：“Even though they're quite confident in themselves, they often find it hard to adapt to these changes.”其中 quite confident in themselves 与选项 D 中的 lack confidence 矛盾，hard to adapt to 替换了选项 E 中的 difficulty in re-adjusting，故选项 D 错误，选项 E 正确。最后听到了由 But on the other hand 引导出的另一正确答案信息：“… many employers find these mature women valuable assets in the workplace.”其中出现了选项 C 中的 mature，故选项 C 正确。选项 B 在录音中导师并未提及。因此答案为 C & E。

25.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 employers；提示词 avoid hiring。
题目解析：题干的条件限定为 avoid hiring，审题时可标画“↓”箭头，重点听负向态度信息。本题定位词 employers 在录音中被替换为 companies，负向态度信息 unwelcoming 符合题干目标信息方向，之后首先听到选项 A 中的 a rival company 出现在了提问中，接着导师的回答是 not a taboo（并非禁忌），即使不知道 taboo 的意思，也可通过随后出现的 always welcomed 这一积极态度信息排除选项 A。接着导师又说：“What concerns employers the most is how they left the previous company … If they have been fired … must have done something wrong …”其中 concerns … the most 呼应题干负向态度信息提示词，fired 替换了选项 C 中的 sacked，故选项 C 正确。同时可排除选项 B，该选项信息不完整，长时间就职于上一家公司不会成为未被雇佣的因素，而离开这家公司的原因才是雇主关心的事情。之后学生的提问中出现了选项 D，注意导师的回答是 isn't at the bottom of the employment list，双重否定表示肯定，即未必不会聘用这类员工，排除选项 D。最后听到：“Most companies cast doubts on applicants who left the job simply because they didn't like …”其中 cast doubts on 呼应题干负向态度信息提示词，left 替换了选项 E 中的 quit，they didn't like the working hours or the work assigned 为 unsuitable 的具体展开替换，故选项 E 正确。因此答案为 C & E。

26.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 employers；提示词 avoid hiring。
题目解析：题干的条件限定为 avoid hiring，审题时可标画“↓”箭头，重点听负向态度信息。本题定位词 employers 在录音中被替换为 companies，负向态度信息 unwelcoming 符合题干目标信息方向，之后首先听到选项 A 中的 a rival company 出现在了提问中，接着导师的回答是 not a taboo（并非禁忌），即使不知道 taboo 的意思，也可通过随后出现的 always welcomed 这一积极态度信息排除选项 A。接着导师又说：“What concerns employers the most is how they left the previous company … If they have been fired … must have done something wrong …”其中 concerns … the most 呼应题干负向态度信息提示词，fired 替换了选项 C 中的 sacked，故选项 C 正确。同时可排除选项 B，该选项信息不完整，长时间就职于上一家公司不会成为未被雇佣的因素，而离开这家公司的原因才是雇主关心的事情。之后学生的提问中出现了选项 D，注意导师的回答是 isn't at the bottom of the employment list，双重否定表示肯定，即未必不会聘用这类员工，排除选项 D。最后听到：“Most companies cast doubts on applicants who left the job simply because they didn't like …”其中 cast doubts on 呼应题干负向态度信息提示词，left 替换了选项 E 中的 quit，they didn't like the working hours or the work assigned 为 unsuitable 的具体展开替换，故选项 E 正确。因此答案为 C & E。

27.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 retrenched workers；提示词 avoid hiring。
题目解析：题干的条件限定为 avoid hiring，审题时可标画“↓”箭头，重点听负向态度信息。导师的提问引出 worker retrenchment 相关信息，但未直接说 avoid hiring 的原因，而是学生先介绍了自己了解的情况并提出问题，负向态度信息 reluctant 才出现在导师的回答中，这时才正式进入主题。首先听到学生提到选项 D 中的 work experience，但以 must have plenty of 这一肯定语气出现，故排除选项 D。学生问在之前行业丰富的工作经验难道不算优势吗？导师的回答中出现了转折信号词 but，注意听之后的内容：“… but retrenched workers know nothing about other industries.”其中 know nothing about other industries 替换了选项 A 的 lack of experience relevant to new industries，故选项 A 正确。随后听到了选项 B 中的 costs，不过注意录音中提到的是 retrain（再培训），与选项 B 中的 employment（雇佣）意思不同，排除选项 B。接着学生提到政府会给这些员工提供培训项目，导师给予了肯定，随后出现了转折信号词 But 提示有特殊情况出现：“But retrenched workers are generally too old.”其中 generally too old 与选项 C 的 comparatively old age 相符，故选项 C 正确。之后又做出了更详细的解释：“They are also reluctant to adjust and are unwilling to be retrained.”其中 reluctant to adjust 和 unwilling to be retrained 符合选项 E 中的 the difficulty of retraining，故选项 E 正确。最后在学生的提问中听到了选项 F 中 reputation 的同义替换 big names，但导师随即给出了否定回答，可排除。选项 G 在这段内容中并未提及。因此答案为 A & C & E。

题目解析：题干的条件限定为 avoid hiring，审题时可标画“↓”箭头，重点听负向态度信息。导师的提问引出 worker retrenchment 相关信息，但未直接说 avoid hiring 的原因，而是学生先介绍了自己了解的情况并提出问题，负向态度信息 reluctant 才出现在导师的回答中，这时才正式进入主题。首先听到学生提到选项 D 中的 work experience，但以 must have plenty of 这一肯定语气出现，故排除选项 D。学生问在之前行业丰富的工作经验难道不算优势吗？导师的回答中出现了转折信号词 but，注意听之后的内容：“… but retrenched workers know nothing about other industries.”其中 know nothing about other industries 替换了选项 A 的 lack of experience relevant to new industries，故选项 A 正确。随后听到了选项 B 中的 costs，不过注意录音中提到的是 retrain（再培训），与选项 B 中的 employment（雇佣）意思不同，排除选项 B。接着学生提到政府会给这些员工提供培训项目，导师给予了肯定，随后出现了转折信号词 But 提示有特殊情况出现：“But retrenched workers are generally too old.”其中 generally too old 与选项 C 的 comparatively old age 相符，故选项 C 正确。之后又做出了更详细的解释：“They are also reluctant to adjust and are unwilling to be retrained.”其中 reluctant to adjust 和 unwilling to be retrained 符合选项 E 中的 the difficulty of retraining，故选项 E 正确。最后在学生的提问中听到了选项 F 中 reputation 的同义替换 big names，但导师随即给出了否定回答，可排除。选项 G 在这段内容中并未提及。因此答案为 A & C & E。

30.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 experience and skills、applicants；提示词 inappropriate，此处应填名词。
题目解析：在听到定位词 experience and skills 出现后，注意抓取符合题干的名词代入。提示词 inappropriate 以反义词形式 appropriate 出现，答案句为：“Wearing appropriate clothes and how they look during an interview are key to a successful interview.”其中 how they look 替换了题干中的 appearance。因此答案为 clothes。
